text.title: AI Feedback Triage Protocol Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules including a `skip-this-methodology` rule. Each pairs a binary check with sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every step in the triage protocol names input artefact, owner, exit criterion, and output location.
rule.rationale: Steps without exit criteria stay open. Steps without owners get done by no one or by everyone simultaneously.

rule.statement: Decision branches are explicit: 'if signal X then lane A, else lane B' with named observable signals (event tag, regex match, threshold), not vibes.
rule.rationale: Implicit branches diverge by operator. Explicit branches converge.

rule.statement: Deviations from the playbook are logged with one-line rationale and surfaced in the next retro.

rationale.source: PDCA / Deming, Out of the Crisis (1986)

rule.rationale: Undocumented deviations destroy institutional memory. Logging makes the playbook self-improving.

rule.statement: Protocol ownership is named at the top — a single accountable person who can authorize amendment.
rule.rationale: Orphaned playbooks rot. Named owners maintain.

rule.statement: Lanes MUST include AI-specific classes (hallucination, refusal, wrong-tool, out-of-scope, cost-spike). Generic 'bug/feature/wontfix' triage is rejected — it loses ML signal.
rule.rationale: Generic product-feedback triage forces ML-relevant signal into 'other' bucket. AI-specific lanes preserve the signal for downstream training data.

rule.statement: Skip when feedback volume <5/week OR no AI-specific failure surface OR generic helpdesk triage already named-owner + exit-criterion compliant.
rule.rationale: Methodology overhead exceeds value below the volume floor; misapplied to non-AI flows it adds taxonomy noise.
